matchups.tacticalAdvantage: Duke enters as the overall No. 1 seed after a 32-2 season. Despite late-season injuries to Caleb Foster, the talent gap anchored by Cameron Boozer is too wide for Siena to overcome.
Ohio State relies heavily on senior guard Bruce Thornton (124.3 AdjO), but TCU brings an elite defense (97.8 AdjD) and enters having won nine of their last 11 games.
Northern Iowa is utterly atrocious on the offensive glass, ranking 356th nationally. St. John's ranks 28th nationally in offensive rebounds and will overpower Northern Iowa with sheer athleticism.

Kansas features potential top NBA pick Darryn Peterson, though minutes limits have been a concern. CBU is making its first NCAA tournament appearance in program history and relies on the fiery scoring of Dominique Daniels.
Louisville is missing primary playmaker Mikel Brown Jr. due to a back injury. South Florida plays at a blistering pace and ranks second nationally in rebounds (43 per game).
Michigan State extended its Big Ten record to 28 consecutive NCAA Tournament appearances. They share the ball exceptionally well and face a Summit League champion in NDSU.
UCF features an explosive offense averaging 81 points per game and plays a tough style. UCLA relies heavily on the production of Tyler Bilodeau and Donovan Dent but has battled uneven performances.
Furman secured a No. 15 seed via the Southern Conference automatic bid and is a dangerous mid-major, but UConn under Dan Hurley is a dynastic mastermind with too much depth.
Arizona opened the season with 23 straight wins and is the model's projected national champion, featuring top-five ranks in both offensive and defensive efficiency.
Villanova's offense is dangerously reliant on three-point variance, and they are missing rotation piece Matt Hodge. Utah State possesses a highly balanced offensive attack.
High Point enters on a fourteen-game winning streak and thrives on offensive execution. Wisconsin ranks 177th nationally in opponent effective field goal percentage.
Arkansas features standout freshman scorer Darius Acuff Jr. and won the SEC Tournament, but sharp money has heavily favored Hawai'i to cover the +15.5 spread.
Texas advanced to the Round of 64 after a gritty win over NC State in the First Four, carrying momentum into a clash against BYU's acclaimed freshman AJ Dybantsa.
Gonzaga secured a No. 3 seed but is considered less dominant than classic Bulldogs teams. They face Conference USA representative Kennesaw State.
Missouri dropped to the 10-line late in the season after struggling down the stretch, drawing a tough, battle-tested ACC opponent in Miami.
Purdue enters at 27-8 after winning the Big Ten Tournament, facing tournament first-timer and Atlantic Sun champion Queens.
Howard advanced out of the First Four, but Michigan boasts the country's most efficient defense (No. 1 AdjD), anchored by 7-foot-7 rim protector Aday Mara.
Georgia leads the nation in fast-break points (19.9 per game). Sharp money heavily backs Georgia due to Saint Louis ranking 230th in offensive turnovers.

Texas Tech lost star forward JT Toppin to a torn ACL, forcing them into a predictable, perimeter-reliant offense. Akron ranks in the top fifteen nationally in both two-point and three-point percentage.

Alabama lost guard Aden Holloway to an indefinite suspension, severely compromising their perimeter spacing. Hofstra plays at a glacial pace (334th nationally) and utilizes a massive frontcourt to exploit Alabama's 292nd-ranked defensive rebounding.
Miami (OH) built a 31-1 record on an artificially inflated, weak schedule. Tennessee is battle-tested, healthy, and will overwhelm Miami with SEC-tier speed and length.
Virginia secured a No. 3 seed with their trademark pack-line defense and will look to suffocate the Horizon League champion.
Kentucky struggles to score in half-court sets and is missing point guard Jaland Lowe (shoulder injury). Santa Clara is well-positioned structurally.
Iowa State relies on the interior dominance of Audi Crooks and boasts incredible defensive metrics. Tennessee State represents the Ohio Valley Conference auto-bid.
Florida is the defending national champion and won the SEC regular-season title. Prairie View advanced out of the First Four, but the talent gap is massive.
Expected to be a severe clash of slow-paced styles. The betting total opened at a first-round low of 129 points. Iowa brings strong March experience.

Vanderbilt is severely underseeded, ranking 12th nationally in KenPom. Vanderbilt shoots 79% from the free-throw line and features physical forwards equipped to exploit McNeese's poor defensive rebounding.
Nebraska heavily regressed late in the season and struggles against elite interior physicality. Troy consistently limits shot volume and efficiency at the basket through elite rim protection.
UNC lost interior enforcer Caleb Wilson to a season-ending hand fracture. VCU's relentless ball pressure is designed to disrupt half-court sets, exploiting UNC's compromised frontcourt.
Illinois possesses the country's most productive attack. Penn arrives hot off an overtime thriller to win the Ivy League tournament under coach Fran McCaffery, but faces a massive talent deficit.
A heavy stylistic clash: Saint Mary's dictates a slow, defensive slog and leads the nation with an 81% free-throw rate, while Texas A&M pushes a fast track meet.
Houston was placed in the South Region, setting up a highly motivated run toward a potential regional final in their hometown at the Toyota Center.
